concerns.text: Phase 7 (Cellular Automata) tasks T045-T052 are listed as a distinct phase but lack explicit dependency markers on the foundational metric logic (US3). Specifically, T048 (Compute CA ground-truth metrics) and T050 (Compute CA reconstruction metrics) depend on the implementation of algorithms in T027-T029 (US3). The current ordering implies Phase 7 can start independently, but T048/T050 cannot execute until US3 logic is complete. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for the metric algorithms.

Task T036 (Implement pipeline orchestration in code/main.py) is placed in Phase 4 (US4), but it orchestrates the flow from Generation (US1) -> Noise (US2) -> Metrics (US3) -> Export (US4). Placing the orchestration task *after* the implementation of the metrics logic (US3) creates a logical gap where the pipeline cannot be tested end-to-end until the very end. While not a hard data-flow violation, the orchestration logic depends on the existence of the functions in US1, US2, and US3. It should be positioned as a 'Integration/Orchestration' task that logically follows the completion of US3, or explicitly noted as dependent on US1-US3 completion before execution.

The note 'Phase 2b (Logic) must complete before User Story implementation' is correct, but the parallel tag [P] on T039-T044 is semantically risky. While file creation is parallel, the logic implementation in T040 (generators) and T041 (noise) might share internal state or configuration loading if not carefully isolated. The [P] tag should be qualified to 'Parallel file creation only; logic implementation requires sequential verification of shared config' to prevent hidden shared state issues during parallel execution.
